title: Accuracy Tracker
description: A simple mod that tells how good your aim is at PVP!
body:
# Accuracy Tracker Mod
## Overview
Accuracy Tracker is a lightweight and strict accuracy tracking mod for Minecraft. It monitors how precisely you keep your crosshair on an entity, helping you refine your aim and improve performance in combat situations, providing accuracy in percentages.

## Features
**Strict Accuracy Tracking: Only records accuracy when the crosshair is directly on an entity's hitbox.**

**Dynamic HUD Display: Displays real-time accuracy at the top-middle of the screen.**

**Color-Coded Accuracy Meter: The HUD smoothly transitions from red (low accuracy) to blue (high accuracy) for easy visualization.**



**Customizable Reset Interval: Configure how long the tracker waits before resetting accuracy calculations. Open the menu through the default keybind "J"**

**Last Accuracy Display: Keeps track of your last recorded accuracy percentage before resetting.**

**Lightweight and Performance-Friendly: Designed to have minimal impact on FPS.**

## ![Download Button Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/338b662de7f60ba486d49d9433c1c4c8341a728e.png)Installation

Install [Fabric Loader](https://fabricmc.net/use/installer/) and [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api).

**Download the latest version of Accuracy Tracker.**

**Place the .jar file into your Minecraft mods folder.**

**Launch the game and start tracking your aim accuracy!**

## Why Use This Mod?

**Whether you're a competitive PvPer, or just curious about your precision in combat, this mod provides an accurate and unforgiving way to measure your crosshair discipline.**

## Compatibilty 

**This mod is built for Fabric and is not compatible with Forge.**

**It does not interfere with other mods and should work alongside other PVP mods.**

## Support

**If you encounter any issues or have feature requests, feel free to reach out or report bugs.**

The mod has reached EOL.
